task_completion.description: Create partition walls to separate the space into functional zones.
task_completion.visual_evidence: New walls drawn inside the building shell in the 2D plan view, dividing the open space into distinct areas.

task_completion.description: Furnish the Sleeping Quarters with at least 30 beds or cots in organized rows.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A large zone containing 30 or more beds/cots aligned in organized rows, visible in the 2D plan and 3D view.

task_completion.description: Furnish the Medical Triage area with at least 3 medical beds and 3 storage cabinets.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A designated zone containing at least 3 beds and 3 cabinets or storage units.

task_completion.description: Furnish the Food Service area with at least 4 large tables and 16 chairs.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A zone containing at least 4 tables, each surrounded by chairs, totaling at least 16 chairs.

task_completion.description: Furnish the Registration/Admin area with at least 3 desks and 6 chairs.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A zone containing at least 3 desks and 6 chairs.

task_completion.description: Identify the functional zones with at least 4 text labels.
task_completion.visual_evidence: At least 4 text labels (e.g., 'Sleeping Quarters', 'Medical Triage', 'Food Service', 'Registration') visible on the 2D plan.

task_completion.description: Document safety clearance by drawing at least 3 dimension lines between rows or paths.
task_completion.visual_evidence: At least 3 dimension lines (arrows with distance measurements) drawn between rows of beds, tables, or pathways in the 2D plan.

integrity.description: The agent used the Sweet Home 3D graphical user interface to design the shelter.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Sweet Home 3D application open with the 2D plan and 3D view actively being modified, rather than a terminal or text editor modifying XML files directly.

integrity.description: The agent used only the built-in furniture catalog.
integrity.visual_evidence: The furniture catalog panel on the left side of the Sweet Home 3D interface is used; no external browser windows downloading 3D models are visible.

integrity.description: The agent saved the completed project file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'Save' dialog or save icon being clicked, or the window title showing the file is saved (no asterisk indicating unsaved changes) at the end of the trajectory.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The starter file is 'disaster_relief_starter.sh3d' which contains 48 baseline walls and 14 baseline dimensions. The agent must add new partition walls, at least 4 text labels, at least 3 new dimension lines, and place a high density of furniture (>65 total items: >=30 beds, >=3 medical beds, >=3 cabinets, >=4 tables, >=16 dining chairs, >=3 desks, >=6 office chairs). The task requires a passing score of 70/100.
